fact: December 25 falls exactly 3 days after the winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere, which means the days are already getting longer again - but only by about 1 minute!
In Antarctica, December 25 happens during their summer, so penguins are actually enjoying 24-hour daylight and temperatures that can reach a toasty 50°F!
December 25 falls during the period when mammals in northern climates naturally produce the most melatonin due to long nights, making cats and other animals seem extra sleepy.
The moon appears to rise about 50 minutes later each night, so whatever moon phase you see on December 25 will look completely different by New Year's Eve.
December 25 is smack in the middle of Capricorn season - people born on this date are supposedly stubborn mountain goats who love climbing to the top of everything!
In northern countries, snow that falls on December 25 creates tiny ice crystals that can actually make a faint tinkling sound when the wind blows through them.
December 25 falls during peak hibernation season, which means bears are literally not pooping for months and their bodies reabsorb all the waste!
The Earth is actually 3 million miles closer to the Sun around December 25 than it is in July - winter happens because of our planet's tilt, not distance!
In Australia and New Zealand, December 25 marks the height of summer, so flowers like jacarandas are blooming purple everywhere while the north freezes.
December 25 sits right in the middle of the time when Earth's magnetic field gets bombarded by extra solar particles, creating more frequent northern lights.
December 25 falls during the season when the jet stream gets super wobbly in North America, causing those crazy temperature swings where it's 70°F one day and snowing the next.
The sunrise on December 25 happens at almost the exact same time as it did on December 21, even though the days are getting longer - sunset is what's actually shifting later!
